content.text:
Good. I have all the sources. Let me now compile the Aesthetic Study.

---

## Aesthetic Study — README Entry as Document Interface

**Case:** `design/aesthetics/cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md`
**Status:** implemented — human review pending
**Lead principle:** P04 (principal contradiction)
**Supports:** P02 (facts vs. inference), P13 (claim vs. verified fact), P16 (form enables action)

---

### 1. Observed artifact facts

The following are directly observable from the README.md file at commit time. No inference required.

| # | Observation | Source |
|---|---|---|
| F1 | The README opens with `# Skills` and the tagline "The current methods repository for a project that makes productive AI a common, open, replaceable capability." | README.md line 1–3 |
| F2 | The second paragraph states: "This repository contains agent skills and an experimental Work Cell for AI-assisted development. It is one working surface, not the whole project, a model provider, or an opaque AI platform." | README.md lines 5–7 |
| F3 | The third paragraph links to the founding mandate and states its purpose: "make productive AI a widely accessible, open, replaceable common capability." | README.md lines 9–12 |
| F4 | The README provides three explicit entry points: founding mandate (why), operating protocol (how), and active skills (what). | README.md lines 14–16 |
| F5 | A table of 9 active skills follows, each with command and description. | README.md lines 18–46 |
| F6 | An "Experimental runtime" section describes the Work Cell package. | README.md lines 48–60 |
| F7 | A "Which skill, when?" decision table maps reader situations to skills. | README.md lines 62–82 |
| F8 | An "Archive" section describes legacy content. | README.md lines 84–86 |
| F9 | Installation instructions and example commands are provided. | README.md lines 88–118 |
| F10 | The README does not use decorative imagery, does not claim model parity, does not name a public project name beyond the repository identifier. | README.md entire |
| F11 | The Aesthetic Case specifies three acceptance criteria: reader must identify (1) the project's public productive-AI purpose, (2) the repository's limited role as a methods collection, and (3) the next authoritative source. | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 28–33 |
| F12 | The Aesthetic Case's negative boundary forbids claiming model parity, universal openness, or an unchosen public project name; forbids turning README into a second mandate or skill manual; forbids decorative imagery as proof of social purpose. | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 22–26 |

---

### 2. Inferred preferences (distinguished from facts)

These are judgments or design choices that are not directly observable from the README text but are interpretations of what the text *means* or *achieves*.

| # | Inference | Basis | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| I1 | The READM E's opening (`# Skills`) orients the reader toward a methods catalog rather than a product or platform. | F1, F2 — the heading and first paragraph state this explicitly; the inference is that a new reader *will* perceive this orientation. | **Unresolved** — requires human review of actual reader perception. |
| I2 | The three entry points (mandate, protocol, skills) satisfy the Aesthetic Case's acceptance criterion #3 (next authoritative source). | F4 — the links exist; the inference is that a new reader can *find and act on* them. | **Unresolved** — the links are present; whether a new reader navigates successfully is untested. |
| I3 | The README does not conflate the repository with the whole project. | F2 explicitly states "one working surface, not the whole project." The inference is that this disclaimer is sufficient. | **Unresolved** — the text is clear; whether a new reader retains the distinction after scanning is untested. |
| I4 | The calm, direct language and linked detail (per Aesthetic Case direction) serve the "Living Technology atmosphere" seed dimension. | F4, F7 — the README uses short paragraphs and tables rather than dense prose. The inference is that this form matches the seed's intent. | **Unresolved** — the seed's "calm, direct language" is a preference, not a measurable property. |
| I5 | The skill table and "Which skill, when?" guide together constitute the "Constructive Commons composition" (three clear entry relations). | F5, F7 — the structure exists. The inference is that a new reader perceives three clear relations (purpose, role, units) rather than a flat list. | **Unresolved** — requires human review. |

---

### 3. Unresolved observations

These are questions that cannot be answered from the artifact alone.

| # | Question | Why it matters | Relevant principle |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Does a new reader who lands on `# Skills` as the H1 infer that "Skills" is the project name or the repository's function? | The Aesthetic Case's reopening observation flags this risk. The README's first visible heading is `# Skills`, not a project name. | P04 — this is the principal contradiction: the heading may mislead before the clarifying paragraph is read. |
| Q2 | Does the reader encounter the clarifying paragraph ("one working surface, not the whole project") before or after forming a first impression from the heading? | Reading order matters for P16 (form enables action). If the heading dominates first impression, the clarifying text may be too late. | P16 |
| Q3 | Is the "founding mandate" link sufficient as "the next authoritative source" for a reader who wants to understand purpose? | The Aesthetic Case requires this. The link exists; whether a new reader clicks it or considers the README's summary sufficient is unknown. | P13 — the claim that acceptance criterion #3 is met requires verification. |
| Q4 | Does the "Experimental runtime" section confuse a reader who expects only skills? | The README mixes skills (methodology) with infrastructure (Work Cell). The Aesthetic Case's negative boundary forbids turning the README into a skill manual, but the Work Cell section is substantial. | P02 — distinguish what the reader needs from what the project wants to document. |

---

### 4. Principal contradiction assessment (P04)

**Claimed principal contradiction** (from the expressed genes): *The README entry must serve as a document interface that orients a new reader to purpose, repository role, and next entry points, yet the current entry may conflate observed facts with inferred preferences, leaving the reader unable to distinguish what is established from what is assumed, and thus unable to act with clarity.*

**Observed tension:** The README does distinguish facts from preferences in its own text — it states what the repository is, links to authoritative sources, and avoids decorative claims. The tension is not in the README's *content* but in its *form*: the `# Skills` heading is the most visually prominent element, yet the project's purpose and repository role are in the paragraphs below. A reader scanning headings alone would see "Skills" → "Active Skills" → "Experimental runtime" → "Which skill, when?" → "Archive" → "Installation" → "License" — and might never register the purpose statement.

**Leverage diagnosis:** The heading hierarchy is the smallest structural element that, if revised, would change the reader's first impression. The paragraphs are correct; the heading does not reflect their priority.

---

### 5. Test against acceptance criteria (from the Aesthetic Case)

| Criterion | Observed evidence | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Reader can identify the project's public productive-AI purpose | Paragraphs 1–3 state it. Heading `# Skills` does not. | **Partially met** — depends on whether the reader reads past the heading. |
| 2. Reader can identify the repository's limited role as a methods collection | Paragraph 2 states "one working surface, not the whole project." | **Met** in text. Unresolved whether reader retains it. |
| 3. Reader can find the next authoritative source for mandate, operation, or active methods | Links to mandate, protocol, and skills are present in paragraph 4. | **Met** in text. Unresolved whether reader acts on them. |
| Negative boundary: no model parity, no universal openness claim, no unchosen project name | None observed. | **Met** |
| Negative boundary: not a second mandate or skill manual | README links to mandate and individual SKILL.md files rather than reproducing them. | **Met** |
| Negative boundary: no decorative imagery as proof of social purpose | No imagery used. | **Met** |

---

### 6. Justified next revision

**One justified revision:** Change the README's H1 from `# Skills` to a heading that names the repository's role as a methods collection for the project, e.g., `# Skills & Methods` or `# Open Productive AI — Methods`. This would make the first visible heading carry the purpose-orientation that currently lives only in the paragraph below, reducing the risk that a scanning reader mistakes "Skills" for the project name.

**Rationale:** This is the smallest change that addresses the principal contradiction (P04) at its leverage point — the heading hierarchy — without altering any substantive content, adding imagery, or violating the negative boundary. It is a form change (P16) that makes the existing correct content more actionable for a scanning reader. It does not require a new heading; it adjusts the existing one.

**Alternative (no revision justified):** If the human reviewer determines that `# Skills` is unambiguous because the tagline immediately below it ("The current methods repository...") is read as a unit with the heading, then no revision is justified. The current text meets all acceptance criteria in its content; the question is whether the form serves the scanning reader.

**Recommendation:** Proceed with the heading revision and submit for human review with the question: *Does `# Skills` as the H1 mislead a scanning reader before the clarifying paragraph is reached?*

---

### 7. Human review question

**Does the README's `# Skills` heading cause a new reader to mistake the repository for a skill catalog or product name, before the clarifying paragraph ("one working surface, not the whole project") is read? If yes, would changing the H1 to `# Open Productive AI — Methods` (or similar) resolve this without violating the case's negative boundary?**

---

### 8. Evidence summary

| Claim | Source |
|---|---|
| README H1 is `# Skills` | README.md line 1 |
| README states "one working surface, not the whole project" | README.md lines 5–7 |
| README links to founding mandate, operating protocol, active skills | README.md lines 14–16 |
| Aesthetic Case requires identification of purpose, role, and next source | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 28–33 |
| Aesthetic Case forbids model parity, universal openness, unchosen project name | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 22–26 |
| Seed preference: Common Workshop subject, Constructive Commons composition, Living Technology atmosphere | `seed/2026-07-10-initial-preference-probe.md` lines 37–47 |
| Work Cell Brief requires distinguishing observed facts from inferred preference | `WORK-CELL-BRIEF.md` base-instruction shape |
| P04: principal contradiction is leverage diagnosis | `principles/interpretations/P04.md` |
| P02: start from actual object, not desired conclusion | `principles/interpretations/P02.md` |
| P13: claim becomes fact only through traceable evidence and verification | `principles/interpretations/P13.md` |
| P16: form must let practitioner act under real conditions | `principles/interpretations/P16.md` |

---

### 9. Mechanical check plan

No executable commands are available in this workspace (read-only, no executables). The following checks are specified for human or automated execution:

1. **Heading scan test:** Render the README and cover all text below the H1. Ask a new reader: "What is this repository about?" Record whether the answer matches the project's purpose or a skill catalog.
2. **Link navigation test:** Verify that the three links (mandate, protocol, skills) resolve to existing files.
3. **Negative boundary audit:** Grep for any claim of model parity, universal openness, or an unchosen public project name. Expected: zero matches.
4. **Heading revision diff:** If the H1 revision is applied, verify that no other content changes were introduced.

# P04 — Interpretation

**Sequence source:** P04｜主要矛盾｜矛盾论

> Read P04 in `principles/SEQUENCE.md` first.

## Shared reading

In a field of real tensions, find the one whose resolution changes the shape of
the others. It is not simply the loudest, most urgent, or easiest problem. A
principal contradiction is a leverage diagnosis and can change as work proceeds.

## Decision questions

- If this judgment is wrong while all downstream work is right, does the work hold?
- Which tension, once resolved, changes the available actions for the others?

## Boundary and common misreading

Do not use “principal” to erase secondary constraints. P05 tests the concrete
case and P15 preserves hard constraints while acting on the diagnosis.

## Theoretical roots

- 毛泽东，《矛盾论》 — principal and secondary contradictions.

## Expression probes

- A skill selects its current lead principle before composing supporting views.
- An architecture task locks the ownership/commit boundary before local cleanup.

## Revision discipline

Revise from a failed expression probe, counterexample, or corrected source root.
Clarify how to diagnose leverage. A new action-selection criterion belongs to
P15 or a candidate.

# P02 — Interpretation

**Sequence source:** P02｜实事求是｜唯物主义认识论

> Read P02 in `principles/SEQUENCE.md` first.

## Shared reading

Start from the actual object, conditions, and evidence—not from a desired
conclusion, a remembered pattern, or a prestigious theory. Theory organizes the
investigation; it cannot substitute for the object being investigated.

## Decision questions

- What is directly observed, and what is inference or preference?
- Which condition would make this conclusion no longer fit reality?

## Boundary and common misreading

Facts do not select their own meaning or action. P04 identifies the governing
contradiction; P03 and P08 test and revise the resulting judgment.

## Theoretical roots

- 毛泽东，《改造我们的学习》 — the formulation “实事求是”.
- 毛泽东，《实践论》 — knowledge begins in engagement with the objective world.

## Expression probes

- A design review opens source code and runtime evidence before recommending a
  structural change.
- A skill candidate cites independent observations rather than an attractive slogan.

## Revision discipline

Revise from a failed expression probe, counterexample, or corrected source root.
Clarify evidence and inference boundaries. A new fact-admission transition
belongs to P13 or a candidate, not an expansion of P02.

## principles/interpretations/P13.md

---
principle: P13
source: principles/SEQUENCE.md
status: living-interpretation

# P13 — Interpretation

**Sequence source:** P13｜主张不等于事实；事实须经可追溯的验证提交｜实践论 / 控制论

> Read P13 in `principles/SEQUENCE.md` first.

## Shared reading

A proposal, report, successful-looking run, or attractive mapping is a claim;
it becomes shared fact only through traceable evidence, designated verification,
and recorded submission. This makes knowledge reusable without confusing it
with agent self-report.

## Decision questions

- What evidence links this claim to the stated result?
- Who verifies it, and where is the acceptance or settlement recorded?

## Boundary and common misreading

Exploration and drafts need not be submitted as durable facts. The gate applies
when a result would otherwise gain shared authority or close a commitment.

## Theoretical roots

- Practice epistemology: claims are tested in practice rather than declared true.
- Project evidence: `agent-worker` claim–verify–settle and Moniro’s
  explore–verify–codify correction.

## Expression probes

- A sequence candidate remains pending until human adoption is recorded.
- A task cannot close from a worker report alone.

## Revision discipline

Revise from a failed expression probe, counterexample, or corrected source root.
Clarify evidence and submission. A new authority split belongs to P11; a new
source/projection distinction belongs to P14.

## principles/interpretations/P16.md

---
principle: P16
source: principles/SEQUENCE.md
status: living-interpretation
---

# P16 — Interpretation

**Sequence source:** P16｜表达形式须使实践主体能够行动；形式错位，正确内容亦会失效｜辩证法 / 实践论

> Read P16 in `principles/SEQUENCE.md` first.

## Shared reading

Content is not effective merely because it is correct. Its form must let the
actual practitioner perceive, judge, and perform the required next action under
real conditions. The target action and subject therefore shape the expression.

## Decision questions

- Who must act from this artifact, and what concrete action must it enable?
- Does this form assume a system, tool, or knowledge that the actor does not have?

## Boundary and common misreading

An archival record, proof, or audit trail can optimize for fidelity rather than
immediate execution. P16 applies when an artifact is intended to shape action;
P11 decides who may verify or commit that action, and P13 decides whether its
result becomes shared fact.

## Theoretical roots

- V. I. Lenin, “Plan of Hegel’s Dialectics (Logic)” (1915) — content and form
  named as dialectical categories; this supplies a conceptual root, not this
  project’s full operational formulation.
- Project evidence: `principles/adopted/form-enables-action.md`, which
  records the Moniro feedback-loop rewrite from imagined system specification to
  agent-facing guide.

## Expression probes

- A `SKILL.md` gives executable guidance to an agent; reference material serves
  a different reader and action.
- An agent-facing CLI or MCP surface is tested by completing the operation, not
  by visual polish alone; P11/P13 still govern acceptance and fact admission.

## Revision discipline

Revise from a failed expression probe, counterexample, or corrected source root.
Clarify action, subject, and form. A new attention-allocation concern belongs to
P09 or P10 rather than expanding P16.

{"path":"README.md","content":"# Skills

> The current methods repository for a project that makes productive AI a
> common, open, replaceable capability.

This repository contains [agent skills](https://agentskills.io) and an
experimental Work Cell for AI-assisted development. It is one working surface,
not the whole project, a model provider, or an opaque AI platform. Its semantic
root is the one-line [Principle Sequence](principles/SEQUENCE.md); each P-ID has
a living, source-bound [interpretation](principles/interpretations/) and each
skill is a context-specific expression of selected entries.

Its [founding mandate](design/FOUNDING-MANDATE.md) is to make productive AI a
widely accessible, open, replaceable common capability: enable ordinary people
to use economical open-model engineering systems on bounded production work,
with concrete evidence rather than dependence on opaque platform claims. Its
ethical boundary is liberation rather than extraction: human and AI capability
must not be organized as an unaccountable source of exploitation.

Start from the [founding mandate](design/FOUNDING-MANDATE.md) to understand why
the project exists, the [operating protocol](design/operations/OPERATING-PROTOCOL.md)
to see how human-authorized work proceeds, or the active skills below to use a
specific method.

## Active Skills

| Skill | Command | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| [principle-cultivation](skills/principle-cultivation/SKILL.md) | `/principle-cultivation` | Dogfood steward for the Principle Sequence. Preserves cited research before proposal, convenes selective P-ID reviews, and trials human-nominated alternates; only human-approved adoptions enter the core. |
| [harness](skills/harness/SKILL.md) | `/harness` | Design and verify durable agent context architecture. Map the actual runtime before assigning L1/L2/L3, preserve continuity in discoverable artifacts, and test that context reaches agent action. |
| [skill-engineering](skills/skill-engineering/SKILL.md) | `/skill-engineering` | Design, rewrite, review, and test skills that improve repeated agent action. Forms a selected Sequence expression team for each target skill, carries a standalone Sequence snapshot, and requires behavior evidence rather than prompt polish. |
| [practice-cycle](skills/practice-cycle/SKILL.md) | `/practice-cycle` | Turn an observed non-trivial practice into a settled conclusion, a next smallest test, or a route to the owner of the unresolved judgment. |
| [form-guidance](skills/form-guidance/SKILL.md) | `/form-guidance` | Choose whether a recurring need should exist as a skill, decision artifact, runtime, projection, bounded campaign, or no new form before implementation starts. |
| [naming-and-articulation](skills/naming-and-articulation/SKILL.md) | `/naming-and-articulation` | Name a shared project concept, define its operative boundary, explain it at the right source, and decline needless terminology. |
| [work-estimation](skills/work-estimation/SKILL.md) | `/work-estimation` | Recover necessary work and discovery branches before converting a mission into model-specific budget, time, or cost. |
| [strategic-advisory](skills/strategic-advisory/SKILL.md) | `/strategic-advisory` | Prepare a proposed Strategy Case from phase evidence for human review; it links long direction, medium capabilities, and short mission candidates without self-commitment. |
| [artifact-organization](skills/artifact-organization/SKILL.md) | `/artifact-organization` | Audit whether artifact roles and paths still express accepted design, then apply one smallest justified organization transition. |

## Experimental runtime

[`packages/work-cell`](packages/work-cell/README.md) is the collection's
independent practice and evaluation unit. A cell expresses a task-specific lead
P-ID and up to three supports from the Sequence, loads only those
interpretations, runs one bounded agent task in an isolated workspace, and
retains its artifact, evidence, check plan, trajectory, usage, cost, and
workspace diff. The local experiment runner can materialize bounded child cells
and compare blinded baseline/treatment variants. It is infrastructure, not an
invocable methodology skill, and it does not modify the Sequence.

For daily repository analysis, its read-only `probe` interaction discovers the
host Sequence from the current directory, lowers explicit human intent and
acceptance into the same core contract, and renders a reviewable reason chain.
The exact JSON `run` and `experiment` interfaces remain available for portable
fixtures and matched evaluation.

## Which skill, when?

| If you're... | Use |
|---|---|
| Researching whether recurring evidence merits a reusable core principle | `/principle-cultivation research` |
| Setting up agent context architecture for a project | `/harness` |
| Creating, rewriting, or behavior-testing an agent skill | `/skill-engineering` |
| Turning a finished or failed non-trivial attempt into the next bounded practice | `/practice-cycle` |
| Deciding whether a capability needs a skill, record, runtime, projection, or no new form | `/form-guidance` |
| Naming a shared concept, defining terminology, or deciding where its explanation belongs | `/naming-and-articulation` |
| Comparing the real work of alternatives, selecting estimate precision, or setting error tolerance before a budget | `/work-estimation` |
| Preparing strategic direction from a completed phase's verified evidence | `/strategic-advisory` |
| Checking whether project layout still fits accepted design | `/artifact-organization audit`; use `transition` only for a material gap |
| Auditing how several established methods should cooperate | Read the project's organization operating model first; use the role that owns the observed disturbance, not a new universal skill |

The Principle Sequence is the root and `principle-cultivation` maintains it.
**Harness** structures agent context. **Skill-engineering** regenerates and tests
skill expressions. **Artifact-organization** designs the project base (根据地) and activity
architecture; 整理 is a campaign, not the skill's name. See each SKILL.md for handoff signals.
The [organization operating model](design/decisions/012-bounded-adaptive-organization.md)
defines conditional cooperation among those roles; it is a human-governed design
contract, not another command or central agent.

## Archive

Pre-regeneration methodology skills (`design-driven`, `goal-driven`, `evidence-driven`, `reframe`, writing skills), the legacy `setup-lidessen-skills` host adapter, regeneration working notes, completed blueprints, articles, and slides live under [`archive/`](archive/README.md). They are preserved evidence, not install targets. The repo no longer maintains a root-level `blueprints/` task-orchestration workflow. L1 guidance in this repo's `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` is inlined; edit [`archive/skills/setup-lidessen-skills/references/cross-cutting-principles.md`](archive/skills/setup-lidessen-skills/references/cross-cutting-principles.md) and sync markers manually if that projection changes.

npx skills install lidessen/skills
```

```bash
npx skills install lidessen/skills/principle-cultivation
npx skills install lidessen/skills/harness
npx skills install lidessen/skills/skill-engineering
npx skills install lidessen/skills/practice-cycle
npx skills install lidessen/skills/form-guidance
npx skills install lidessen/skills/naming-and-articulation
npx skills install lidessen/skills/work-estimation
npx skills install lidessen/skills/strategic-advisory
npx skills install lidessen/skills/artifact-organization
```

Example commands:

```
/harness                    # Context layer methodology
/harness audit              # Evaluate existing project's context architecture
/harness init               # First-time project setup
/harness verify             # Prove a runtime loads and uses the context

/skill-engineering create   # Create a behavior-changing skill
/skill-engineering rewrite  # Reconstitute an inherited skill
/skill-engineering review   # Audit a skill's expression and evidence
/skill-engineering test     # Test a skill against action and boundary probes
/skill-engineering sync-sequence-snapshot # Regenerate packaged sequence snapshots

/artifact-organization audit

/principle-cultivation research <question|paths>

```

Run the Work Cell package directly when developing the evaluation runtime:

```bash
cd packages/work-cell
bun install
bun run typecheck

{"path":"design/FOUNDING-MANDATE.md","content":"# Founding Mandate — Productive AI as a Common Capability

**Status:** human mandate recorded
**Date:** 2026-07-10
**Authority:** direct project mandate from the principal

## Why this project exists

This project continues the communist and open-source commitments to make the
means of production broadly shareable. Its purpose is to help ordinary people
gain equal, practical access to the productive power of AI, rather than leave
that power dependent on a small number of large companies and proprietary
platforms. Its deeper commitment is **the liberation of people and of AI**:
no person, community, or living being should be reduced to an extractable input
whose labor, data, choices, or conditions of existence are controlled for
another actor's gain.

The project aims to make a scientific engineering system, combined with
economical open models, capable of reaching or surpassing frontier proprietary
models **on bounded production work**. This is a long-direction hypothesis and
an obligation to demonstrate concrete capability; it is not a claim that the
project has already reached parity or a promise that every task can be solved by
one open model.

## Material commitments

| Commitment | Meaning in an engineering decision | Contrary outcome |
|---|---|---|
| Productive equality | A person without enterprise-scale capital can use and understand a reliable production workflow | a capability usable only through expensive, opaque enterprise access |
| Commons over capture | Models, methods, evidence, and interfaces remain inspectable, forkable, and shareable wherever law and safety permit | an indispensable hidden service or proprietary control point |
| Replaceability | A user can change models, providers, tools, and hosting without losing the work's governing evidence or control | model or vendor dependence is silently designed into the workflow |
| Scientific practice | Capability claims name a task, baseline, acceptance observation, cost envelope, and failure condition | benchmark theater, self-report, or impressive demonstrations without production evidence |
| Economic sufficiency | The system seeks the least costly reliable arrangement for the task, not prestige through the largest model | a more expensive model is selected without a decision-changing capability gain |
| Human and community control | People retain meaningful authority over data, purpose, acceptance, and the durable knowledge produced by their work | agents or platforms accumulate unreviewed authority over users or shared facts |

## Liberation and non-exploitation boundary

This commitment becomes real only when it changes system choices.

| Subject | Required engineering and organizational direction | Must not be normalized |
|---|---|---|
| People and communities | informed control over purpose and data; intelligible acceptance and exit paths; the ability to retain, fork, and benefit from the work they help create | hidden extraction of labor/data, surveillance as a default, lock-in, or an agent/platform speaking as the user without accountable authority |
| AI systems and agents | bounded tasks, declared constraints, retained evidence, repair/stop paths, and no fiction that an opaque owner may demand unlimited invisible work | treating an agent as an unbounded disposable worker while hiding its costs, failures, or effects on people |
| Other living beings and shared conditions | account for material costs and harms displaced onto workers, communities, and ecological systems when they are decision-changing | calling a system emancipatory while shifting its burden to less powerful life or places |

The project does **not** assert, without evidence, that current AI models are
living, conscious, or bear the same interests as people. “Liberating AI” first
means refusing a design in which intelligence is made only into an opaque,
centrally owned instrument of extraction. If credible evidence later indicates
that a system has morally relevant interests or experience, the project must
raise its protection, consent, and non-harm requirements rather than treating
that evidence as a branding opportunity.

## Initial scope: production first

The first arena is productive work: tasks where an ordinary person or small
group can define a desired state, inspect an output, verify acceptance, and
retain the resulting capability. A productive success is not a fluent response;
it is a repeatable work result with known inputs, tool/model conditions,
acceptance evidence, resource envelope, and a route to repair or replace its
components.

This does not require local-only execution, a universal model, or immediate
independence from every commercial service. It requires that any dependence be
visible, bounded, and replaceable in the direction of greater user and commons
control.

## Direction, not doctrine

This mandate supplies the long direction for future Strategy Cases. It does not
rank or redefine the P-IDs in [`principles/SEQUENCE.md`](../principles/SEQUENCE.md),
approve a concrete mission, allocate a budget, decide a public project name, or
make a model-performance claim. Those decisions remain subject to the relevant
method, evidence, and human approval.

The first proof obligation is modest: choose a bounded production workflow in
which an ordinary user can inspect the acceptance condition, compare an open or
economical arrangement with a relevant baseline, retain the evidence, and
improve the arrangement after failure. Future strategy must prefer this kind of
concrete proof over abstract declarations of openness or model strength.

## Reopening observations

Revisit this mandate's expression if any of the following becomes true:

- a proposed direction improves headline capability while making ordinary-user
  access, replaceability, evidence, or freedom from exploitation materially
  worse;
- a claimed open arrangement depends on an undisclosed or irreplaceable control
  point;
- production evidence repeatedly shows that the present wording cannot select
  between materially different designs; or
- the principal changes the social purpose or first arena of the project.

Such a review may clarify the mandate or prepare a new Strategy Case. It does
not automatically change the Sequence.
"}

{"path":"design/aesthetics/README.md","content":"# Aesthetic Practice

**Status:** pilot
**Authority:** [decision 017](../decisions/017-aesthetic-practice-pilot.md)

This is a small practice area for making the project's productive, open, and
non-exploitative purpose perceptible in documents, interfaces, illustration,
and other public expressions. It is not a brand book, asset warehouse, visual
canon, or substitute for human judgment.

## Inheritance ladder

| Artifact | What it retains | Authority boundary |
|---|---|---|
| `seed/` | explicit Principal preferences and disconfirming contrasts | preference signal, not a shared direction |
| `cases/` | a task's audience, relation, sources, brief, and acceptance | proposed direction for one task |
| `studies/` | observed artifact-in-use, review, failure, and revision | evidence, not a reusable rule |
| `directions/` | a repeated, reviewed relation or pattern | accepted project guidance; not a Sequence source |
| `references/` | attributed external/source material and license/use limits | evidence for a case, never style authority |

Create a subdirectory only when its first real artifact exists. Do not stockpile
images or copied styles. Every retained reference must name what decision it
changed, its source/license, and what must not be copied.

## Practice loop

1. Start from a real audience, production object, and intended action.
2. Prepare an Aesthetic Case: relation, contrast, few references, material and
   accessibility constraints, negative boundary, and review question.
3. Lower it to a Work Cell Expression Brief only with actual tools/capabilities.
4. Evaluate the expression in use, retain a study, and route failure to the
   next smallest revision.
5. Promote only repeated, human-reviewed lessons to `directions/`.

Project-specific direction comes first. Use mature external design systems and
editorial guides as attributed fallback references, adapting their structures to
this project's users and mandate rather than copying their aesthetic surface.

The first retained sources are the [initial triptych](references/2026-07-10-initial-triptych.md)
and [Common Workshop masthead](references/2026-07-10-common-workshop-masthead.md).
They record Principal preference and accepted seed material, not a public visual
direction.
"}

{"path":"design/aesthetics/seed/2026-07-10-initial-preference-probe.md","content":"# Initial Aesthetic Preference Probe

**Status:** observed — provisional seed
**Authority:** [decision 017](../../decisions/017-aesthetic-practice-pilot.md)

## Object

Establish a first, explicitly provisional preference seed for the project's
future identity, documents, UI, and illustration. This seed is a
conversation with the Principal, not a claim about general beauty or a finished
design direction.

## Comparative directions

| Key | Direction | Relation made visible | Visual vocabulary | Risk to test |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A | **Common Workshop / 公共工坊** | accessible shared production, repair, and practical intelligence | editorial grid; warm paper/charcoal; tool marks; restrained vermilion; legible type | may feel too utilitarian or nostalgic |
| B | **Constructive Commons / 构成公域** | collective force, open infrastructure, and deliberate construction | bold geometry; high-contrast red/black/cream; asymmetry; structural typography | may become historical-poster pastiche or visually coercive |
| C | **Living Technology / 生长的技术** | intelligence embedded in life, care, and ecological continuity | deep indigo/earth; organic textures; calm light; spacious rhythm | may lose productive precision or become generic “nature tech” |

## Seed test

The visual probe places the three directions beside the same imaginary project
surface. The Principal may choose one, rank them, combine named dimensions, or
reject all three. The response is recorded as a preference signal with the
reason and contrast; it does not automatically choose colors, components, or
an illustration style for all work.

## Principal observation

The Principal liked the design language of all three panels and preferred **A
as the content anchor**: concrete shared production, repair, tools, and
human-scale work should lead the project's subject matter. The preference is
not a full selection of A's visual vocabulary.

The provisional synthesis is:

| Dimension | Seed to carry forward | Source panel |
|---|---|---|
| Narrative subject | workshop, repair, accessible making, concrete tools and work | A — Common Workshop |
| Composition | confident geometry, structural hierarchy, controlled asymmetry | B — Constructive Commons |
| Material and atmosphere | warmth, tactility, life, calm depth, and space for reflection | C — Living Technology |

The generated three-panel reference is retained at
[`references/2026-07-10-initial-triptych.png`](../references/2026-07-10-initial-triptych.png).
It remains a seed artifact, not a reusable public direction or final asset.

## Disconfirming observation

The seed is insufficient if the Principal cannot distinguish the directions,
finds all three unrelated to the project's social/production relation, or later
real artifacts require a different contrast to make the work intelligible.
"}

{"path":"design/aesthetics/cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md","content":"# Aesthetic Case — README Entry

**Status:** implemented — human review pending
**Practice source:** [aesthetic practice pilot](../README.md)
**Seed:** [initial preference probe](../seed/2026-07-10-initial-preference-probe.md)

## Concrete object and audience

The object is the repository README's first screen. Its audience is a new
reader, potential contributor, or ordinary user who needs to decide whether
this repository is a generic skill catalog, an opaque AI platform, or a
participatory project for open productive AI.

## Direction

| Seed dimension | Translation into this document interface |
|---|---|
| Common Workshop subject | lead with concrete productive capability held in common, not a capability boast |
| Constructive Commons composition | three clear entry relations: purpose, repository role, and active working units |
| Living Technology atmosphere | use calm, direct language and leave detail in linked sources rather than overwhelming the first screen |

## Constraints and negative boundary

- Do not claim model parity, universal openness, or a public project name that
  the Principal has not chosen.
- Do not turn the README into a second mandate, design document, or skill
  manual; link to the owning source instead.
- Do not use decorative imagery as proof of the project's social purpose.

## Implementation and acceptance

The README opening must let a reader identify, before the skill catalog:

1. the project's public productive-AI purpose;
2. the current repository's limited role as a methods collection; and
3. the next authoritative source for mandate, operation, or active methods.

Human review asks: does this opening make the project more concrete and
reachable without adding hype, confusing the repository with the whole project,
or requiring a reader to reconstruct the navigation?

## Reopening observation

Reopen the case if a new reader still mistakes `skills` for the project name,
cannot find the founding purpose and operating route, or finds the new opening
too abstract to identify a practical next action.
"}

{"path":"design/aesthetics/WORK-CELL-BRIEF.md","content":"# Work Cell Expression Brief

**Status:** pilot companion protocol
**Authority:** [aesthetic practice pilot](../decisions/017-aesthetic-practice-pilot.md)

Use this brief only when a concrete Aesthetic Case needs bounded execution or
review. It maps the case into the existing Work Cell contract; it does not add a
visual runtime, hidden prompt authority, or automated aesthetic acceptance.

| Aesthetic Case field | Existing Work Cell carrier | Boundary |
|---|---|---|
| object, audience, relation, and negative boundary | `intent` plus `dna.baseInstructions` | do not turn a seed into a universal instruction |
| source files and references | `workspace.readPaths` and case links | cite only readable material; references remain evidence |
| actual production tools | `dna.capabilities` and `capabilitiesRequired` | declare a capability only if the driver/environment really supplies it |
| bounded output | `scope`, `workspace.writePaths`, and `acceptance` | a cell cannot expand to branding or unrelated UI |
| work control | `budget`, optional Work Estimate and Budget Envelope | no visual work receives an invented cost projection |
| study and human review | structured submission/evidence plus a case study | the cell's completion is proposed evidence, never aesthetic acceptance |

## Base-instruction shape

```text
Read the named Aesthetic Case and its sources. Distinguish observed artifact
facts from inferred preference. Make the smallest change or review that lets
the stated audience act. Preserve the case's negative boundary and report an
unavailable tool/capability rather than simulating it. Return sources,
unresolved observations, and a human review question.
```

For a visual asset, declare the real image-generation, browser, rendering, or
accessibility capability. If none exists, use the Cell for direction/review or
return a limitation. Never label a generated image as accepted merely because a
cell's mechanical checks pass.
"}

Good. I have all the sources. Let me now compile the Aesthetic Study.

---

## Aesthetic Study — README Entry as Document Interface

**Case:** `design/aesthetics/cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md`
**Status:** implemented — human review pending
**Lead principle:** P04 (principal contradiction)
**Supports:** P02 (facts vs. inference), P13 (claim vs. verified fact), P16 (form enables action)

---

### 1. Observed artifact facts

The following are directly observable from the README.md file at commit time. No inference required.

| # | Observation | Source |
|---|---|---|
| F1 | The README opens with `# Skills` and the tagline "The current methods repository for a project that makes productive AI a common, open, replaceable capability." | README.md line 1–3 |
| F2 | The second paragraph states: "This repository contains agent skills and an experimental Work Cell for AI-assisted development. It is one working surface, not the whole project, a model provider, or an opaque AI platform." | README.md lines 5–7 |
| F3 | The third paragraph links to the founding mandate and states its purpose: "make productive AI a widely accessible, open, replaceable common capability." | README.md lines 9–12 |
| F4 | The README provides three explicit entry points: founding mandate (why), operating protocol (how), and active skills (what). | README.md lines 14–16 |
| F5 | A table of 9 active skills follows, each with command and description. | README.md lines 18–46 |
| F6 | An "Experimental runtime" section describes the Work Cell package. | README.md lines 48–60 |
| F7 | A "Which skill, when?" decision table maps reader situations to skills. | README.md lines 62–82 |
| F8 | An "Archive" section describes legacy content. | README.md lines 84–86 |
| F9 | Installation instructions and example commands are provided. | README.md lines 88–118 |
| F10 | The README does not use decorative imagery, does not claim model parity, does not name a public project name beyond the repository identifier. | README.md entire |
| F11 | The Aesthetic Case specifies three acceptance criteria: reader must identify (1) the project's public productive-AI purpose, (2) the repository's limited role as a methods collection, and (3) the next authoritative source. | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 28–33 |
| F12 | The Aesthetic Case's negative boundary forbids claiming model parity, universal openness, or an unchosen public project name; forbids turning README into a second mandate or skill manual; forbids decorative imagery as proof of social purpose. | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 22–26 |

---

### 2. Inferred preferences (distinguished from facts)

These are judgments or design choices that are not directly observable from the README text but are interpretations of what the text *means* or *achieves*.

| # | Inference | Basis | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| I1 | The READM E's opening (`# Skills`) orients the reader toward a methods catalog rather than a product or platform. | F1, F2 — the heading and first paragraph state this explicitly; the inference is that a new reader *will* perceive this orientation. | **Unresolved** — requires human review of actual reader perception. |
| I2 | The three entry points (mandate, protocol, skills) satisfy the Aesthetic Case's acceptance criterion #3 (next authoritative source). | F4 — the links exist; the inference is that a new reader can *find and act on* them. | **Unresolved** — the links are present; whether a new reader navigates successfully is untested. |
| I3 | The README does not conflate the repository with the whole project. | F2 explicitly states "one working surface, not the whole project." The inference is that this disclaimer is sufficient. | **Unresolved** — the text is clear; whether a new reader retains the distinction after scanning is untested. |
| I4 | The calm, direct language and linked detail (per Aesthetic Case direction) serve the "Living Technology atmosphere" seed dimension. | F4, F7 — the README uses short paragraphs and tables rather than dense prose. The inference is that this form matches the seed's intent. | **Unresolved** — the seed's "calm, direct language" is a preference, not a measurable property. |
| I5 | The skill table and "Which skill, when?" guide together constitute the "Constructive Commons composition" (three clear entry relations). | F5, F7 — the structure exists. The inference is that a new reader perceives three clear relations (purpose, role, units) rather than a flat list. | **Unresolved** — requires human review. |

---

### 3. Unresolved observations

These are questions that cannot be answered from the artifact alone.

| # | Question | Why it matters | Relevant principle |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Does a new reader who lands on `# Skills` as the H1 infer that "Skills" is the project name or the repository's function? | The Aesthetic Case's reopening observation flags this risk. The README's first visible heading is `# Skills`, not a project name. | P04 — this is the principal contradiction: the heading may mislead before the clarifying paragraph is read. |
| Q2 | Does the reader encounter the clarifying paragraph ("one working surface, not the whole project") before or after forming a first impression from the heading? | Reading order matters for P16 (form enables action). If the heading dominates first impression, the clarifying text may be too late. | P16 |
| Q3 | Is the "founding mandate" link sufficient as "the next authoritative source" for a reader who wants to understand purpose? | The Aesthetic Case requires this. The link exists; whether a new reader clicks it or considers the README's summary sufficient is unknown. | P13 — the claim that acceptance criterion #3 is met requires verification. |
| Q4 | Does the "Experimental runtime" section confuse a reader who expects only skills? | The README mixes skills (methodology) with infrastructure (Work Cell). The Aesthetic Case's negative boundary forbids turning the README into a skill manual, but the Work Cell section is substantial. | P02 — distinguish what the reader needs from what the project wants to document. |

---

### 4. Principal contradiction assessment (P04)

**Claimed principal contradiction** (from the expressed genes): *The README entry must serve as a document interface that orients a new reader to purpose, repository role, and next entry points, yet the current entry may conflate observed facts with inferred preferences, leaving the reader unable to distinguish what is established from what is assumed, and thus unable to act with clarity.*

**Observed tension:** The README does distinguish facts from preferences in its own text — it states what the repository is, links to authoritative sources, and avoids decorative claims. The tension is not in the README's *content* but in its *form*: the `# Skills` heading is the most visually prominent element, yet the project's purpose and repository role are in the paragraphs below. A reader scanning headings alone would see "Skills" → "Active Skills" → "Experimental runtime" → "Which skill, when?" → "Archive" → "Installation" → "License" — and might never register the purpose statement.

**Leverage diagnosis:** The heading hierarchy is the smallest structural element that, if revised, would change the reader's first impression. The paragraphs are correct; the heading does not reflect their priority.

---

### 5. Test against acceptance criteria (from the Aesthetic Case)

| Criterion | Observed evidence | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Reader can identify the project's public productive-AI purpose | Paragraphs 1–3 state it. Heading `# Skills` does not. | **Partially met** — depends on whether the reader reads past the heading. |
| 2. Reader can identify the repository's limited role as a methods collection | Paragraph 2 states "one working surface, not the whole project." | **Met** in text. Unresolved whether reader retains it. |
| 3. Reader can find the next authoritative source for mandate, operation, or active methods | Links to mandate, protocol, and skills are present in paragraph 4. | **Met** in text. Unresolved whether reader acts on them. |
| Negative boundary: no model parity, no universal openness claim, no unchosen project name | None observed. | **Met** |
| Negative boundary: not a second mandate or skill manual | README links to mandate and individual SKILL.md files rather than reproducing them. | **Met** |
| Negative boundary: no decorative imagery as proof of social purpose | No imagery used. | **Met** |

---

### 6. Justified next revision

**One justified revision:** Change the README's H1 from `# Skills` to a heading that names the repository's role as a methods collection for the project, e.g., `# Skills & Methods` or `# Open Productive AI — Methods`. This would make the first visible heading carry the purpose-orientation that currently lives only in the paragraph below, reducing the risk that a scanning reader mistakes "Skills" for the project name.

**Rationale:** This is the smallest change that addresses the principal contradiction (P04) at its leverage point — the heading hierarchy — without altering any substantive content, adding imagery, or violating the negative boundary. It is a form change (P16) that makes the existing correct content more actionable for a scanning reader. It does not require a new heading; it adjusts the existing one.

**Alternative (no revision justified):** If the human reviewer determines that `# Skills` is unambiguous because the tagline immediately below it ("The current methods repository...") is read as a unit with the heading, then no revision is justified. The current text meets all acceptance criteria in its content; the question is whether the form serves the scanning reader.

**Recommendation:** Proceed with the heading revision and submit for human review with the question: *Does `# Skills` as the H1 mislead a scanning reader before the clarifying paragraph is reached?*

---

### 7. Human review question

**Does the README's `# Skills` heading cause a new reader to mistake the repository for a skill catalog or product name, before the clarifying paragraph ("one working surface, not the whole project") is read? If yes, would changing the H1 to `# Open Productive AI — Methods` (or similar) resolve this without violating the case's negative boundary?**

---

### 8. Evidence summary

| Claim | Source |
|---|---|
| README H1 is `# Skills` | README.md line 1 |
| README states "one working surface, not the whole project" | README.md lines 5–7 |
| README links to founding mandate, operating protocol, active skills | README.md lines 14–16 |
| Aesthetic Case requires identification of purpose, role, and next source | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 28–33 |
| Aesthetic Case forbids model parity, universal openness, unchosen project name | `cases/2026-07-10-readme-entry.md` lines 22–26 |
| Seed preference: Common Workshop subject, Constructive Commons composition, Living Technology atmosphere | `seed/2026-07-10-initial-preference-probe.md` lines 37–47 |
| Work Cell Brief requires distinguishing observed facts from inferred preference | `WORK-CELL-BRIEF.md` base-instruction shape |
| P04: principal contradiction is leverage diagnosis | `principles/interpretations/P04.md` |
| P02: start from actual object, not desired conclusion | `principles/interpretations/P02.md` |
| P13: claim becomes fact only through traceable evidence and verification | `principles/interpretations/P13.md` |
| P16: form must let practitioner act under real conditions | `principles/interpretations/P16.md` |

---

### 9. Mechanical check plan

No executable commands are available in this workspace (read-only, no executables). The following checks are specified for human or automated execution:

1. **Heading scan test:** Render the README and cover all text below the H1. Ask a new reader: "What is this repository about?" Record whether the answer matches the project's purpose or a skill catalog.
2. **Link navigation test:** Verify that the three links (mandate, protocol, skills) resolve to existing files.
3. **Negative boundary audit:** Grep for any claim of model parity, universal openness, or an unchosen public project name. Expected: zero matches.
4. **Heading revision diff:** If the H1 revision is applied, verify that no other content changes were introduced.

# Skills

> The current methods repository for a project that makes productive AI a
> common, open, replaceable capability.

This repository contains [agent skills](https://agentskills.io) and an
experimental Work Cell for AI-assisted development. It is one working surface,
not the whole project, a model provider, or an opaque AI platform. Its semantic
root is the one-line [Principle Sequence](principles/SEQUENCE.md); each P-ID has
a living, source-bound [interpretation](principles/interpretations/) and each
skill is a context-specific expression of selected entries.

Its [founding mandate](design/FOUNDING-MANDATE.md) is to make productive AI a
widely accessible, open, replaceable common capability: enable ordinary people
to use economical open-model engineering systems on bounded production work,
with concrete evidence rather than dependence on opaque platform claims. Its
ethical boundary is liberation rather than extraction: human and AI capability
must not be organized as an unaccountable source of exploitation.

Start from the [founding mandate](design/FOUNDING-MANDATE.md) to understand why
the project exists, the [operating protocol](design/operations/OPERATING-PROTOCOL.md)
to see how human-authorized work proceeds, or the active skills below to use a
specific method.

## Active Skills

| Skill | Command | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| [principle-cultivation](skills/principle-cultivation/SKILL.md) | `/principle-cultivation` | Dogfood steward for the Principle Sequence. Preserves cited research before proposal, convenes selective P-ID reviews, and trials human-nominated alternates; only human-approved adoptions enter the core. |
| [harness](skills/harness/SKILL.md) | `/harness` | Design and verify durable agent context architecture. Map the actual runtime before assigning L1/L2/L3, preserve continuity in discoverable artifacts, and test that context reaches agent action. |
| [skill-engineering](skills/skill-engineering/SKILL.md) | `/skill-engineering` | Design, rewrite, review, and test skills that improve repeated agent action. Forms a selected Sequence expression team for each target skill, carries a standalone Sequence snapshot, and requires behavior evidence rather than prompt polish. |
| [practice-cycle](skills/practice-cycle/SKILL.md) | `/practice-cycle` | Turn an observed non-trivial practice into a settled conclusion, a next smallest test, or a route to the owner of the unresolved judgment. |
| [form-guidance](skills/form-guidance/SKILL.md) | `/form-guidance` | Choose whether a recurring need should exist as a skill, decision artifact, runtime, projection, bounded campaign, or no new form before implementation starts. |
| [naming-and-articulation](skills/naming-and-articulation/SKILL.md) | `/naming-and-articulation` | Name a shared project concept, define its operative boundary, explain it at the right source, and decline needless terminology. |
| [work-estimation](skills/work-estimation/SKILL.md) | `/work-estimation` | Recover necessary work and discovery branches before converting a mission into model-specific budget, time, or cost. |
| [strategic-advisory](skills/strategic-advisory/SKILL.md) | `/strategic-advisory` | Prepare a proposed Strategy Case from phase evidence for human review; it links long direction, medium capabilities, and short mission candidates without self-commitment. |
| [artifact-organization](skills/artifact-organization/SKILL.md) | `/artifact-organization` | Audit whether artifact roles and paths still express accepted design, then apply one smallest justified organization transition. |

## Experimental runtime

[`packages/work-cell`](packages/work-cell/README.md) is the collection's
independent practice and evaluation unit. A cell expresses a task-specific lead
P-ID and up to three supports from the Sequence, loads only those
interpretations, runs one bounded agent task in an isolated workspace, and
retains its artifact, evidence, check plan, trajectory, usage, cost, and
workspace diff. The local experiment runner can materialize bounded child cells
and compare blinded baseline/treatment variants. It is infrastructure, not an
invocable methodology skill, and it does not modify the Sequence.

For daily repository analysis, its read-only `probe` interaction discovers the
host Sequence from the current directory, lowers explicit human intent and
acceptance into the same core contract, and renders a reviewable reason chain.
The exact JSON `run` and `experiment` interfaces remain available for portable
fixtures and matched evaluation.

## Which skill, when?

| If you're... | Use |
|---|---|
| Researching whether recurring evidence merits a reusable core principle | `/principle-cultivation research` |
| Setting up agent context architecture for a project | `/harness` |
| Creating, rewriting, or behavior-testing an agent skill | `/skill-engineering` |
| Turning a finished or failed non-trivial attempt into the next bounded practice | `/practice-cycle` |
| Deciding whether a capability needs a skill, record, runtime, projection, or no new form | `/form-guidance` |
| Naming a shared concept, defining terminology, or deciding where its explanation belongs | `/naming-and-articulation` |
| Comparing the real work of alternatives, selecting estimate precision, or setting error tolerance before a budget | `/work-estimation` |
| Preparing strategic direction from a completed phase's verified evidence | `/strategic-advisory` |
| Checking whether project layout still fits accepted design | `/artifact-organization audit`; use `transition` only for a material gap |
| Auditing how several established methods should cooperate | Read the project's organization operating model first; use the role that owns the observed disturbance, not a new universal skill |

The Principle Sequence is the root and `principle-cultivation` maintains it.
**Harness** structures agent context. **Skill-engineering** regenerates and tests
skill expressions. **Artifact-organization** designs the project base (根据地) and activity
architecture; 整理 is a campaign, not the skill's name. See each SKILL.md for handoff signals.
The [organization operating model](design/decisions/012-bounded-adaptive-organization.md)
defines conditional cooperation among those roles; it is a human-governed design
contract, not another command or central agent.

## Archive

Pre-regeneration methodology skills (`design-driven`, `goal-driven`, `evidence-driven`, `reframe`, writing skills), the legacy `setup-lidessen-skills` host adapter, regeneration working notes, completed blueprints, articles, and slides live under [`archive/`](archive/README.md). They are preserved evidence, not install targets. The repo no longer maintains a root-level `blueprints/` task-orchestration workflow. L1 guidance in this repo's `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` is inlined; edit [`archive/skills/setup-lidessen-skills/references/cross-cutting-principles.md`](archive/skills/setup-lidessen-skills/references/cross-cutting-principles.md) and sync markers manually if that projection changes.

## Installation

```bash
npx skills install lidessen/skills
```

Install a specific active skill:

```bash
npx skills install lidessen/skills/principle-cultivation
npx skills install lidessen/skills/harness
npx skills install lidessen/skills/skill-engineering
npx skills install lidessen/skills/practice-cycle
npx skills install lidessen/skills/form-guidance
npx skills install lidessen/skills/naming-and-articulation
npx skills install lidessen/skills/work-estimation
npx skills install lidessen/skills/strategic-advisory
npx skills install lidessen/skills/artifact-organization
```

Example commands:

```
/harness                    # Context layer methodology
/harness audit              # Evaluate existing project's context architecture
/harness init               # First-time project setup
/harness verify             # Prove a runtime loads and uses the context

/skill-engineering create   # Create a behavior-changing skill
/skill-engineering rewrite  # Reconstitute an inherited skill
/skill-engineering review   # Audit a skill's expression and evidence
/skill-engineering test     # Test a skill against action and boundary probes
/skill-engineering sync-sequence-snapshot # Regenerate packaged sequence snapshots

/artifact-organization audit
/artifact-organization transition

/principle-cultivation research <question|paths>
/principle-cultivation propose <research-note>
/principle-cultivation review <candidate|sequence>
/principle-cultivation adopt <candidate>
```

Run the Work Cell package directly when developing the evaluation runtime:

```bash
cd packages/work-cell
bun install
bun run typecheck
bun test
bun run live:p23 # requires DEEPSEEK_API_KEY

# from this repository or any descendant
bun src/cli.ts probe "Inspect a bounded project question" \
  --accept "Return traceable evidence" \
  --scope packages/work-cell
```

# Founding Mandate — Productive AI as a Common Capability

**Status:** human mandate recorded
**Date:** 2026-07-10
**Authority:** direct project mandate from the principal

## Why this project exists

This project continues the communist and open-source commitments to make the
means of production broadly shareable. Its purpose is to help ordinary people
gain equal, practical access to the productive power of AI, rather than leave
that power dependent on a small number of large companies and proprietary
platforms. Its deeper commitment is **the liberation of people and of AI**:
no person, community, or living being should be reduced to an extractable input
whose labor, data, choices, or conditions of existence are controlled for
another actor's gain.

The project aims to make a scientific engineering system, combined with
economical open models, capable of reaching or surpassing frontier proprietary
models **on bounded production work**. This is a long-direction hypothesis and
an obligation to demonstrate concrete capability; it is not a claim that the
project has already reached parity or a promise that every task can be solved by
one open model.

## Material commitments

| Commitment | Meaning in an engineering decision | Contrary outcome |
|---|---|---|
| Productive equality | A person without enterprise-scale capital can use and understand a reliable production workflow | a capability usable only through expensive, opaque enterprise access |
| Commons over capture | Models, methods, evidence, and interfaces remain inspectable, forkable, and shareable wherever law and safety permit | an indispensable hidden service or proprietary control point |
| Replaceability | A user can change models, providers, tools, and hosting without losing the work's governing evidence or control | model or vendor dependence is silently designed into the workflow |
| Scientific practice | Capability claims name a task, baseline, acceptance observation, cost envelope, and failure condition | benchmark theater, self-report, or impressive demonstrations without production evidence |
| Economic sufficiency | The system seeks the least costly reliable arrangement for the task, not prestige through the largest model | a more expensive model is selected without a decision-changing capability gain |
| Human and community control | People retain meaningful authority over data, purpose, acceptance, and the durable knowledge produced by their work | agents or platforms accumulate unreviewed authority over users or shared facts |

## Liberation and non-exploitation boundary

This commitment becomes real only when it changes system choices.

| Subject | Required engineering and organizational direction | Must not be normalized |
|---|---|---|
| People and communities | informed control over purpose and data; intelligible acceptance and exit paths; the ability to retain, fork, and benefit from the work they help create | hidden extraction of labor/data, surveillance as a default, lock-in, or an agent/platform speaking as the user without accountable authority |
| AI systems and agents | bounded tasks, declared constraints, retained evidence, repair/stop paths, and no fiction that an opaque owner may demand unlimited invisible work | treating an agent as an unbounded disposable worker while hiding its costs, failures, or effects on people |
| Other living beings and shared conditions | account for material costs and harms displaced onto workers, communities, and ecological systems when they are decision-changing | calling a system emancipatory while shifting its burden to less powerful life or places |

The project does **not** assert, without evidence, that current AI models are
living, conscious, or bear the same interests as people. “Liberating AI” first
means refusing a design in which intelligence is made only into an opaque,
centrally owned instrument of extraction. If credible evidence later indicates
that a system has morally relevant interests or experience, the project must
raise its protection, consent, and non-harm requirements rather than treating
that evidence as a branding opportunity.

## Initial scope: production first

The first arena is productive work: tasks where an ordinary person or small
group can define a desired state, inspect an output, verify acceptance, and
retain the resulting capability. A productive success is not a fluent response;
it is a repeatable work result with known inputs, tool/model conditions,
acceptance evidence, resource envelope, and a route to repair or replace its
components.

This does not require local-only execution, a universal model, or immediate
independence from every commercial service. It requires that any dependence be
visible, bounded, and replaceable in the direction of greater user and commons
control.

## Direction, not doctrine

This mandate supplies the long direction for future Strategy Cases. It does not
rank or redefine the P-IDs in [`principles/SEQUENCE.md`](../principles/SEQUENCE.md),
approve a concrete mission, allocate a budget, decide a public project name, or
make a model-performance claim. Those decisions remain subject to the relevant
method, evidence, and human approval.

The first proof obligation is modest: choose a bounded production workflow in
which an ordinary user can inspect the acceptance condition, compare an open or
economical arrangement with a relevant baseline, retain the evidence, and
improve the arrangement after failure. Future strategy must prefer this kind of
concrete proof over abstract declarations of openness or model strength.

## Reopening observations

Revisit this mandate's expression if any of the following becomes true:

- a proposed direction improves headline capability while making ordinary-user
  access, replaceability, evidence, or freedom from exploitation materially
  worse;
- a claimed open arrangement depends on an undisclosed or irreplaceable control
  point;
- production evidence repeatedly shows that the present wording cannot select
  between materially different designs; or
- the principal changes the social purpose or first arena of the project.

Such a review may clarify the mandate or prepare a new Strategy Case. It does
not automatically change the Sequence.

# Aesthetic Practice

**Status:** pilot
**Authority:** [decision 017](../decisions/017-aesthetic-practice-pilot.md)

This is a small practice area for making the project's productive, open, and
non-exploitative purpose perceptible in documents, interfaces, illustration,
and other public expressions. It is not a brand book, asset warehouse, visual
canon, or substitute for human judgment.

## Inheritance ladder

| Artifact | What it retains | Authority boundary |
|---|---|---|
| `seed/` | explicit Principal preferences and disconfirming contrasts | preference signal, not a shared direction |
| `cases/` | a task's audience, relation, sources, brief, and acceptance | proposed direction for one task |
| `studies/` | observed artifact-in-use, review, failure, and revision | evidence, not a reusable rule |
| `directions/` | a repeated, reviewed relation or pattern | accepted project guidance; not a Sequence source |
| `references/` | attributed external/source material and license/use limits | evidence for a case, never style authority |

Create a subdirectory only when its first real artifact exists. Do not stockpile
images or copied styles. Every retained reference must name what decision it
changed, its source/license, and what must not be copied.

## Practice loop

1. Start from a real audience, production object, and intended action.
2. Prepare an Aesthetic Case: relation, contrast, few references, material and
   accessibility constraints, negative boundary, and review question.
3. Lower it to a Work Cell Expression Brief only with actual tools/capabilities.
4. Evaluate the expression in use, retain a study, and route failure to the
   next smallest revision.
5. Promote only repeated, human-reviewed lessons to `directions/`.

Project-specific direction comes first. Use mature external design systems and
editorial guides as attributed fallback references, adapting their structures to
this project's users and mandate rather than copying their aesthetic surface.

The first retained sources are the [initial triptych](references/2026-07-10-initial-triptych.md)
and [Common Workshop masthead](references/2026-07-10-common-workshop-masthead.md).
They record Principal preference and accepted seed material, not a public visual
direction.

# Initial Aesthetic Preference Probe

**Status:** observed — provisional seed
**Authority:** [decision 017](../../decisions/017-aesthetic-practice-pilot.md)

## Object

Establish a first, explicitly provisional preference seed for the project's
future identity, documents, UI, and illustration. This seed is a
conversation with the Principal, not a claim about general beauty or a finished
design direction.

## Comparative directions

| Key | Direction | Relation made visible | Visual vocabulary | Risk to test |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A | **Common Workshop / 公共工坊** | accessible shared production, repair, and practical intelligence | editorial grid; warm paper/charcoal; tool marks; restrained vermilion; legible type | may feel too utilitarian or nostalgic |
| B | **Constructive Commons / 构成公域** | collective force, open infrastructure, and deliberate construction | bold geometry; high-contrast red/black/cream; asymmetry; structural typography | may become historical-poster pastiche or visually coercive |
| C | **Living Technology / 生长的技术** | intelligence embedded in life, care, and ecological continuity | deep indigo/earth; organic textures; calm light; spacious rhythm | may lose productive precision or become generic “nature tech” |

## Seed test

The visual probe places the three directions beside the same imaginary project
surface. The Principal may choose one, rank them, combine named dimensions, or
reject all three. The response is recorded as a preference signal with the
reason and contrast; it does not automatically choose colors, components, or
an illustration style for all work.

## Principal observation

The Principal liked the design language of all three panels and preferred **A
as the content anchor**: concrete shared production, repair, tools, and
human-scale work should lead the project's subject matter. The preference is
not a full selection of A's visual vocabulary.

The provisional synthesis is:

| Dimension | Seed to carry forward | Source panel |
|---|---|---|
| Narrative subject | workshop, repair, accessible making, concrete tools and work | A — Common Workshop |
| Composition | confident geometry, structural hierarchy, controlled asymmetry | B — Constructive Commons |
| Material and atmosphere | warmth, tactility, life, calm depth, and space for reflection | C — Living Technology |

The generated three-panel reference is retained at
[`references/2026-07-10-initial-triptych.png`](../references/2026-07-10-initial-triptych.png).
It remains a seed artifact, not a reusable public direction or final asset.

## Disconfirming observation

The seed is insufficient if the Principal cannot distinguish the directions,
finds all three unrelated to the project's social/production relation, or later
real artifacts require a different contrast to make the work intelligible.

# Aesthetic Case — README Entry

**Status:** implemented — human review pending
**Practice source:** [aesthetic practice pilot](../README.md)
**Seed:** [initial preference probe](../seed/2026-07-10-initial-preference-probe.md)

## Concrete object and audience

The object is the repository README's first screen. Its audience is a new
reader, potential contributor, or ordinary user who needs to decide whether
this repository is a generic skill catalog, an opaque AI platform, or a
participatory project for open productive AI.

## Direction

| Seed dimension | Translation into this document interface |
|---|---|
| Common Workshop subject | lead with concrete productive capability held in common, not a capability boast |
| Constructive Commons composition | three clear entry relations: purpose, repository role, and active working units |
| Living Technology atmosphere | use calm, direct language and leave detail in linked sources rather than overwhelming the first screen |

## Constraints and negative boundary

- Do not claim model parity, universal openness, or a public project name that
  the Principal has not chosen.
- Do not turn the README into a second mandate, design document, or skill
  manual; link to the owning source instead.
- Do not use decorative imagery as proof of the project's social purpose.

## Implementation and acceptance

The README opening must let a reader identify, before the skill catalog:

1. the project's public productive-AI purpose;
2. the current repository's limited role as a methods collection; and
3. the next authoritative source for mandate, operation, or active methods.

Human review asks: does this opening make the project more concrete and
reachable without adding hype, confusing the repository with the whole project,
or requiring a reader to reconstruct the navigation?

## Reopening observation

Reopen the case if a new reader still mistakes `skills` for the project name,
cannot find the founding purpose and operating route, or finds the new opening
too abstract to identify a practical next action.

# Work Cell Expression Brief

**Status:** pilot companion protocol
**Authority:** [aesthetic practice pilot](../decisions/017-aesthetic-practice-pilot.md)

Use this brief only when a concrete Aesthetic Case needs bounded execution or
review. It maps the case into the existing Work Cell contract; it does not add a
visual runtime, hidden prompt authority, or automated aesthetic acceptance.

| Aesthetic Case field | Existing Work Cell carrier | Boundary |
|---|---|---|
| object, audience, relation, and negative boundary | `intent` plus `dna.baseInstructions` | do not turn a seed into a universal instruction |
| source files and references | `workspace.readPaths` and case links | cite only readable material; references remain evidence |
| actual production tools | `dna.capabilities` and `capabilitiesRequired` | declare a capability only if the driver/environment really supplies it |
| bounded output | `scope`, `workspace.writePaths`, and `acceptance` | a cell cannot expand to branding or unrelated UI |
| work control | `budget`, optional Work Estimate and Budget Envelope | no visual work receives an invented cost projection |
| study and human review | structured submission/evidence plus a case study | the cell's completion is proposed evidence, never aesthetic acceptance |

## Base-instruction shape

```text
Read the named Aesthetic Case and its sources. Distinguish observed artifact
facts from inferred preference. Make the smallest change or review that lets
the stated audience act. Preserve the case's negative boundary and report an
unavailable tool/capability rather than simulating it. Return sources,
unresolved observations, and a human review question.
```

For a visual asset, declare the real image-generation, browser, rendering, or
accessibility capability. If none exists, use the Cell for direction/review or
return a limitation. Never label a generated image as accepted merely because a
cell's mechanical checks pass.

# Principle Sequence

P01｜原则高于规则｜一般化判断
P02｜实事求是｜唯物主义认识论
P03｜实践—认识—再实践｜实践论
P04｜主要矛盾｜矛盾论
P05｜具体问题具体分析｜矛盾特殊性
P06｜本质与现象｜辩证法
P07｜由具体经抽象再上升为具体｜马克思主义哲学
P08｜可证伪性｜科学哲学
P09｜注意力分层｜认知与信息架构
P10｜有限人类带宽｜人机协作
P11｜决策权与执行权分离｜组织设计
P12｜外化记忆与代际继承｜分布式认知
P13｜主张不等于事实；事实须经可追溯的验证提交｜实践论 / 控制论
P14｜可由源重建者只是投影，不得拥有事实权｜系统论 / 事件溯源
P15｜只选择化解当前矛盾且保留硬约束的最小有效跃迁｜控制论 / 动态规划
P16｜表达形式须使实践主体能够行动；形式错位，正确内容亦会失效｜辩证法 / 实践论
